channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: North Korea displays new long-range missile at a military parade celebrating Kim Jong Un
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — At a massive military parade attended by foreign leaders, North Korean leader Kim Jong Un rolled out his nuclear-armed military’s most powerful weapons, including a new intercontinental ballistic missile he may be preparing to test in coming weeks.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG and HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: A Sudanese paramilitary attack kills at least 53 people in Darfur, an aid group says
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — A shelling and drone attack by the Sudanese paramilitary forces hit a shelter in a besieged city in the Darfur region, killing at least 53 people, a doctors’ group said Saturday. The onslaught was the latest in Sudan's more than two-year war.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: ATF deploys elite team after deadly Tennessee explosion; search on for 18 missing
item.description: Authorities said 18 remain missing after a deadly explosion at Accurate Energetic Systems' plant in Tennessee killed multiple workers Friday morning.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: These states approved permanent daylight saving time: Why their clocks still change in November
item.description: These states have all affirmed that they want to observe daylight saving time year-round, yet their clocks will switch to standard time in November with the rest of us. Here's why.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, Fox 5

item.title: Pro-Palestinian protesters march to stadium ahead of World Cup qualifier between Norway and Israel
item.description: OSLO, Norway (AP) — Pro-Palestinian protesters lit flares and waved flags as they marched to the stadium ahead of the World Cup qualifier between Norway and Israel on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Biden is receiving radiation and hormone therapy to treat his prostate cancer
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Former President Joe Biden is receiving radiation and hormone therapy as part of a new phase of treating the aggressive form of prostate cancer he was diagnosed with after leaving office, a spokesperson said Saturday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Luigi Mangione's lawyers seek dismissal of federal charges in assassination of UnitedHealthcare CEO
item.description: **Video is previous coverage of Luigi Mangione pleading not guilty to charges in December 2024** NEW YORK (AP) — Lawyers for Luigi Mangione asked a New York federal judge Saturday to dismiss some criminal charges, including the only count for which he could face the death penalty, from a federal ind
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, KREX 5

item.title: 7 risk factors of deadly fungus infections identified, new Candida auris research finds
item.description: Researchers are getting a better idea of what leads to clinical cases of Candida auris, a dangerous and drug-resistant fungus that has been rapidly multiplying.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, KREX 5

item.title: Mississippi homecoming turns deadly: 4 killed, 12 injured in downtown Leland shooting
item.description: A shooting in downtown Leland, Mississippi, during a high school homecoming weekend left four people dead and 12 other wounded, Fox News Digital learned.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: OSHA violations reported prior to massive explosion at Tennessee facility: documents
item.description: As investigators continue to comb through the wreckage of an explosion in Tennessee that left 18 people unaccounted for Friday, Nexstar's WKRN has learned more about the company that operated the facility.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Wilson, WFLA, NBC

item.title: A look at how Venezuelans in the US are reacting to Maria Corina Machado's Nobel Prize win
item.description: Venezuelans in the U.S. are welcoming the news that opposition leader leader Maria Corina Machado won the Nobel Peace Price.
item.dc:creator: Gisela Salomon, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: New Orleans is electing a new mayor and a wide field is pledging change after indictments
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — New Orleans voters on Saturday are picking a new mayor to replace two-term incumbent LaToya Cantrell, whose indictment on corruption charges has left a wide field of candidates promising change to city hall.
item.dc:creator: JACK BROOK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Blast at a Tennessee explosives plant leaves 18 missing and feared dead, sheriff says
item.description: McEWEN, Tenn. (AP) — A blast leveled an explosives plant Friday in rural Tennessee, leaving behind a mass of twisted metal, burned-out shells of cars and at least 18 people missing and feared dead, authorities said. “There’s nothing to describe.
item.dc:creator: TRAVIS LOLLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pentagon will build a training facility for Qatari pilots in Idaho
item.description: The U.S. military is moving forward with plans to build a dedicated facility in Idaho to train pilots from Qatar, an important U.S. ally in the Middle East, Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth announced Friday.
item.dc:creator: KONSTANTIN TOROPIN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: WIC food program receives $300M to keep running during government shutdown
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A food aid program that supports millions of low-income mothers and their young children received a $300 million infusion from the Trump administration this week, alleviating some anxiety that it would run out of money during the government shutdown.
item.dc:creator: MORIAH BALINGIT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Two jurors claim they were bullied into convicting Harvey Weinstein and regret it, his lawyers say
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Two jurors who voted in June to convict Harvey Weinstein of sexual assault said they regret the decision and only did so because others on the panel bullied them, the former movie mogul’s lawyers said in a newly public court filing.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump's quest for the Nobel Peace Prize falls short again despite high-profile nominations
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump was passed over for the Nobel Peace Prize on Friday despite jockeying from his fellow Republicans, various world leaders and — most vocally — himself.
item.dc:creator: MIKE PESOLI and MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Nor'easter without a name to bring ocean flooding and high winds to US East Coast
item.description: A storm without a name was expected to bring flooding to the East Coast from South Carolina to New Jersey and high winds to New York City over the holiday weekend. While the storm affecting the eastern U.S.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: National Guard stands down in Illinois while troops patrol in Memphis with local police
item.description: MEMPHIS, Tenn. (AP) — National Guard troops patrolled in Tennessee's second-largest city for the first time Friday while soldiers in Illinois were engaged only in planning and training after a judge handed a loss to the Trump administration and blocked their deployment to the Chicago area.
item.dc:creator: ADRIAN SAINZ and CHRISTINE FERNANDO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: WGN employee detained amid ICE activity in Chicago, later released
item.description: An employee of WGN-TV was detained by two Border Patrol officers and later released after federal agents swarmed a busy intersection in Chicago Friday Morning.
item.dc:creator: Christine Flores, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump threatens tech export limits, new 100% tariff on Chinese imports starting Nov. 1 or sooner
item.description: Trump said that “starting November 1st, 2025 (or sooner, depending on any further actions or changes taken by China), the United States of America will impose a Tariff of 100% on China, over and above any Tariff that they are currently paying.
item.dc:creator: Fox 5

item.title: WGN employee detained amid ICE activity on Chicago's North Side, later released
item.description: Witnesses who captured the incident on video spoke to WGN about the moments leading up to the staffer's detainment.
item.dc:creator: Christine Flores, NewsNation

item.title: Las Vegas Jewish community expresses hope, hesitation on peace deal between Israel, Hamas
item.description: Israel and Hamas are taking a major step toward ending their two-year-long war. The new deal calls for Hamas to release all hostages and for Israel to pull back its forces from Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Jezzamine Wolk, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: US is sending about 200 troops to Israel to help support and monitor the Gaza ceasefire deal
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The United States is sending about 200 troops to Israel to help support and monitor the ceasefire deal in Gaza as part of a team that includes partner nations, nongovernmental organizations and private-sector players, U.S. officials said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: KONSTANTIN TOROPIN and MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Judge blocks National Guard deployment in Illinois for 2 weeks
item.description: A judge on Thursday blocked the deployment of National Guard troops in the Chicago area for at least two weeks, finding no substantial evidence that a “danger of rebellion” is brewing in Illinois during Trump's immigration crackdown.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTINE FERNANDO and SUDHIN THANAWALA, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Tennessee teacher fired over Charlie Kirk posts files free speech lawsuit
item.description: A longtime Rutherford County teacher is suing her school district, claiming she was fired for what she shared online.
item.dc:creator: Kelly Milan, ABC 4

item.title: Judge to feds: Don’t use crowd control weapons on Chicago media
item.description: A suit filed earlier this week by news outlets and media associations, among others, said federal agents used “indiscriminate” force and interfered with freedoms of the press.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: Judge temporarily blocks Trump National Guard deployment to Chicago area
item.description: A federal judge temporarily blocked President Trump’s federalization and deployment of hundreds of National Guard members throughout Illinois.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Saying 'I do' to AI? Ohio lawmaker proposes ban on marriage, legal personhood for AI
item.description: “As the computer systems improve in their capacity to act more like humans, we want to be sure we have prohibitions in our law that prohibit those systems from ever being human in their agency.
item.dc:creator: Ava Boldizar, NewsNation

item.title: Rhode Island dentist helps save peregrine falcon with broken beak
item.description: "The keratin from the base that's damaged is going to grow back," Sheida Soleimani said. "So we did a kind of bridge with dental epoxy.
item.dc:creator: Raymond Baccari, NewsNation

item.title: CDC panel to review childhood vaccine schedules
item.description: The vaccine advisory panel for the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) is planning to look into the timing of other childhood vaccine schedules, soon after it voted to delay the measles, mumps, rubella and chickenpox vaccine.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, Fox 5

item.title: Last of 10 New Orleans jail escapees agrees to return to Louisiana after Atlanta capture
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — New Orleans jail escapee Derrick Groves, who spent nearly five months on the run before being captured in Georgia, agreed to be returned to Louisiana in a Thursday court appearance.
item.dc:creator: JACK BROOK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Arizona sheriff's office misused millions meant to remedy racial profiling, report reveals
item.description: PHOENIX (AP) — The sheriff’s office for metro Phoenix spent millions of dollars budgeted for compliance costs in a racial profiling case over Joe Arpaio’s immigration crackdowns on things that had little or nothing to do with a court-ordered overhaul of the agency, according to an expert’s report.
item.dc:creator: JACQUES BILLEAUD, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Judge orders suspect in deadly LA wildfire to remain jailed, citing mental health concerns
item.description: ORLANDO, Fla. (AP) — A federal judge in Florida ordered the man charged in California's deadly Palisades Fire to remain jailed Thursday after a prosecutor said he had traits of an arsonist and his family had worried about his declining mental state.
item.dc:creator: MIKE SCHNEIDER and CHRISTOPHER WEBER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Woman fatally shot outside PetSmart after dispute over not saying 'thank you': officials
item.description: A woman in Texas was shot and killed after an argument over not saying "thank you" for opening the door, police revealed in an arrest affidavit.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump was nominated for the Nobel Peace Prize before. Experts say he's unlikely to win on Friday
item.description: Trump’s bid to win the Nobel Peace Prize has drawn added attention to the annual guessing game over who its next laureate will be.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Israel, Hamas agree to 'first phase' of plan to end fighting, release hostages, Trump says
item.description: Israel and Hamas have agreed to the “first phase” of his peace plan to pause fighting and release at least some hostages and prisoners, U.S. President Donald Trump said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5
